Our story

We built SEQYO after
getting breached.

Our founders experienced firsthand how fragmented, expensive, and slow traditional security tools are. SEQYO was born from that frustration — a platform that’s fast, unified, and puts expert humans at its core.

The problem we set out to solve

The average company uses 76 different security tools. They don’t talk to each other, they generate thousands of false positives, and when something real happens — it takes days to figure out what.

Meanwhile, cybercriminals operate with startling efficiency. They share tools, sell exploits, and collaborate in ways the enterprise world struggles to match.

SEQYO changes that equation. We give companies a unified intelligence layer — powered by AI, backed by experts — that’s as fast and collaborative as the threats they face.

[Replace with team photo]

What we stand for

The principles that guide every decision we make.

Security-first

We build what we preach. SEQYO itself undergoes continuous third-party penetration testing, holding SOC 2 Type II and ISO 27001 certifications.

Human-centered

Technology amplifies human judgment — it never replaces it. Every feature is designed to make security professionals more effective, not obsolete.

Radically transparent

No black boxes. We explain every alert, every decision, and every recommendation in plain language your whole team can understand.

Relentlessly fast

In security, speed is survival. Our entire platform is engineered for sub-second detection, instant alerting, and 60-second expert dispatch.

Leadership team

Decades of combined experience protecting the world’s most critical systems.

DRK
Dr. Rachel Kim
CEO & Co-Founder

Former NSA Cybersecurity Director. 18 years securing critical infrastructure.

MD
Marco Deluca
CTO & Co-Founder

Previously led security engineering at two Fortune 500 cloud providers.

AO
Amara Osei
Chief Security Officer

CISSP, ex-Mandiant IR lead. Has responded to 200+ major breaches.

YT
Yuki Tanaka
Head of AI Research

PhD in adversarial ML. Published 14 papers on AI-driven threat detection.
